title: Why it might be time for a slumping Bruins team to call up James Hagens
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are struggling with offense, scoring only three goals in their last three games.
James Hagens, a 19-year-old forward, could be considered for an NHL call-up to boost the Bruins' lineup.
Hagens currently has four points in six AHL games with the Providence Bruins, showcasing strong passing and power-play skills.

title: Tatum, Celtics pull away late to beat Raptors, move closer to 2-seed: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum led the Boston Celtics to a 113-95 win over the Toronto Raptors, showcasing improved passing and rebounding.
Neemias Queta contributed significantly, scoring 18 points, and continues to be a contender for the Most Improved Player award.
Nikola Vucevic returned from injury, playing 13 minutes, as the Celtics overcame turnovers and poor 3-point shooting to secure the victory.

title: New Bedford man gets decades-long prison sentence for raping young relative
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Daniel Turcios Torres, 46, received a 36-45 year prison sentence for raping a young relative repeatedly over five years.
The assaults began after the victim, 12 at the time, moved to the U.S. from Ecuador with family in 2019.
Turcios Torres faces charges including child rape, indecent assault, and witness intimidation; post-prison, he faces immigration detention.

title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes, 23, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her mother, who died of brain cancer.
Oakes' mother was diagnosed with Stage 4 brain cancer, living three and a half years due to MGH's cancer care.
Following her mother's death, Oakes became an oncology nurse, working in ICU-level cancer care for nearly two years.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man allegedly in a mental health crisis wielding a sword near Northeastern University.
The man stabbed a police officer and attacked an EMS clinician before being shot by responding officers.
Multiple officers and two emergency responders sustained non-life-threatening injuries in the incident.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23, from Hudson, NH, is running the Boston Marathon in 2026 to honor her late godmother Diana.
Diana received care at Massachusetts General Hospital before passing from brain cancer in November 2024.
Dubois aims to support families facing cancer by raising funds for MGH's Cancer Care Equity program.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A woman in her 40s died in a Brockton house fire early Saturday morning.
Two individuals were hospitalized following the fire at 80 Glendale Ave, officials report.
The fire started in a second-floor bedroom; its cause is under investigation by multiple agencies.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, of Raymond, NH, was killed in a shootout with police on April 4, 2026.
Masse allegedly shot and wounded a Nottingham officer after firing at family members earlier the same day.
An investigation is ongoing, with police gathering body and cruiser camera footage from the incident.

title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon, a breast cancer survivor from Canton, Mass., will run the Boston Marathon on April 20, 2026.
Gannon's best friend Peggy, also a cancer survivor, will join her, both running for Mass General Hospital.
The pair hope to inspire others battling cancer by sharing their marathon journey of survivorship and friendship.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted 'SNL' for the fifth time on April 4, 2026, joining the five-timers club.
Musical guest Jack White performed on 'SNL' for the fifth time, showcasing two musical acts.
The episode featured Black and White collaborating on an 'SNL'-ified version of 'Seven Nation Army.'
